title: dryopteris guatemalensis
intro: Dryopteris guatemalensis, also known as Guatemalan wood fern, is a species of fern native to Central America, characterized by its lance-shaped fronds and dark green color. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dryopteris guatemalensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and protect against infections, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Dryopteris guatemalensis can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or poultice, allowing for various herbal preparations to be made from its leaves and stems.
